title: Incident Learning Loop
summary: Converts an incident into concrete prevention, detection, and response improvements.
steps: Reconstruct the incident timeline from alerts, logs, deploys, human actions, and customer impact.
Separate contributing factors from blame and identify where detection, prevention, or response failed.
Choose the highest-leverage follow-up that reduces recurrence or time to recovery.
Implement and verify that follow-up, then update runbooks or alerts.
Review whether the same pattern exists elsewhere.
checks: at least one prevention or detection improvement ships
stopConditions: top learning item is implemented and verified
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Incident Learning Loop for [incident, timeline, service].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- Reconstruct the incident timeline from alerts, logs, deploys, human actions, and customer impact.
- Separate contributing factors from blame and identify where detection, prevention, or response failed.
- Choose the highest-leverage follow-up that reduces recurrence or time to recovery.
- Implement and verify that follow-up, then update runbooks or alerts.
- Review whether the same pattern exists elsewhere.

Quality gates before completion:
- timeline is evidence-based
- follow-ups have owners
- at least one prevention or detection improvement ships
- runbook updated

Stop when one of these is true:
- top learning item is implemented and verified
- follow-ups require cross-team prioritization
- owner accepts remaining risk

Finish with:
- timeline
- contributing factor list
- follow-up diff
- verification output

Escalate for human approval before: customer communications, SLO changes, policy changes.
